reasoning: The Lean statement correctly encodes each component of the informal claim: `DifferentiableOn ℝ f (Ioo a b)` matches "f differentiable on (a,b)"; the quantifiers over x₁, x₂ ∈ Ioo a b match "for any two points in (a,b)"; `Ioo (min x₁ x₂) (max x₁ x₂)` correctly formalizes "ξ between x₁ and x₂" (strictly, in the standard MVT reading); and `f x₁ - f x₂ = (x₁ - x₂) * deriv f ξ` faithfully encodes the equation f(x₁) - f(x₂) = (x₁ - x₂)f'(ξ). The equation is symmetric under swapping x₁ and x₂ so the orientation via min/max is natural. The only subtle point is the degenerate x₁ = x₂ case, where the Ioo is empty — but the informal statement is also literally false there (there is no strict "between"), so both formulations handle this degenerate case identically. The encoding mirrors Mathlib's `exists_deriv_eq_slope` style.
